appId.label: App ID
appId.description: The app_id of your Intercom app which will indicate where to store any data.

activator.label: Custom Inbox Button Selector
activator.description: By default, Intercom will inject their own inbox button onto the page, but you can choose to use your own custom button instead by providing a CSS selector, e.g. #my-button. You must have the "Show the Intercom Inbox" setting enabled for this to work. The default value is #IntercomDefaultWidget.

richLinkProperties.label: Rich Link Properties
richLinkProperties.description: A list of rich link property keys.

apiBase.label: Regional Data Hosting
apiBase.description: The regional API to use for processing the data

revenue.label: Revenue
revenue.description: The amount associated with a purchase. Segment will multiply by 100 as Intercom requires the amount in cents.

currency.label: Currency
currency.description: The currency of the purchase amount. Segment will default to USD if revenue is provided without a currency.

identifyUser.title: Identify User
identifyUser.description: Create or update a user in Intercom.

user_id.label: User ID
user_id.description: A unique identifier for the user.

language_override.label: Language Override
language_override.description: The user's messenger language (instead of relying on browser language settings).

created_at.label: User Creation Time
created_at.description: The time the user was created in your system.

avatar_image_url.label: Avatar
avatar_image_url.description: The URL for the user's avatar/profile image.

user_hash.label: User Hash
user_hash.description: The user hash used for identity verification. See [Intercom docs](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/collections/12295815-messenger-security) for more information on how to set this field.

intercom_user_jwt.label: Intercom User JWT
intercom_user_jwt.description: The intercom user JWT is used to secure your messenger for your users. See [Intercom docs](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/collections/12295815-messenger-security) for more information on how to set this field.

company_id.label: Company ID
company_id.description: The unique identifier of the company.

created_at.label: Company Creation Time
created_at.description: The time the company was created in your system.

plan.label: Company Plan
plan.description: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

monthly_spend.label: Monthly Spend
monthly_spend.description: The monthly spend of the company, e.g. how much revenue the company generates for your business.

size.label: Company Size
size.description: The number of employees in the company.

website.label: Company Website
website.description: The URL for the company website.

industry.label: Industry
industry.description: The industry that the company operates in.

company_custom_traits.label: Company Custom Attributes
company_custom_traits.description: The custom attributes for the company object.

companies.label: Companies
companies.description: The array of companies the user is associated to.

company_id.label: Company ID
company_id.description: The unique identifier of the company.

created_at.label: Company Creation Time
created_at.description: The time the company was created in your system.

plan.label: Company Plan
plan.description: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

monthly_spend.label: Monthly Spend
monthly_spend.description: The monthly spend of the company, e.g. how much revenue the company generates for your business.

size.label: Company Size
size.description: The number of employees in the company.

website.label: Company Website
website.description: The URL for the company website.

industry.label: Industry
industry.description: The industry that the company operates in.

company_custom_traits.label: Company Custom Attributes
company_custom_traits.description: The custom attributes for the company object.

hide_default_launcher.label: Hide Default Launcher
hide_default_launcher.description: Selectively show the chat widget. As per [Intercom docs](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/articles/189-turn-off-show-or-hide-the-intercom-messenger), you want to first hide the Messenger for all users inside the Intercom UI using Messenger settings. Then think about how you want to programmatically decide which users you would like to show the widget to.

created_at.description: The time the company was created in your system.

plan.label: Company Plan

monthly_spend.description: The monthly spend of the company, e.g. how much revenue the company generates for your business.

industry.label: Industry

company_custom_traits.description: The custom attributes for the company object.

hide_default_launcher.label: Hide Default Launcher
hide_default_launcher.description: Selectively show the chat widget. As per [Intercom docs](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/articles/189-turn-off-show-or-hide-the-intercom-messenger), you want to first hide the Messenger for all users inside the Intercom UI using Messenger settings. Then think about how you want to programmatically decide which users you would like to show the widget to.
